message:
style: apply rustfmt import reordering across macros, source, tests, benches, and conformance crate

Enforce consistent import ordering via rustfmt across all 138 modified
files in the workspace:

- asupersync-macros/src/ (join, race, scope, session, spawn, util):
  reorder proc_macro and syn imports to standard grouping
- benches/ (14 files): alphabetize benchmark imports, group external
  crate imports before internal module imports
- conformance/src/ (bench, lean_coverage_matrix, lean_frontier, lib,
  runner, tests/*, traceability): standardize import blocks
- examples/ (6 files): consistent import ordering
- franken_decision/src/lib.rs, franken_evidence/src/lib.rs + tests:
  reorder imports
- src/bin/asupersync.rs: reorder binary entry point imports
- tests/ (30+ files): alphabetize and group test imports consistently

Pure formatting change — no functional modifications. Net -66 lines
from consolidating multi-line import blocks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
 //! Lean coverage matrix schema validation tests (bd-13aa6).
 
 use conformance::{
-    CoverageRowType, CoverageStatus, LEAN_COVERAGE_SCHEMA_VERSION, LeanCoverageMatrix,
+    CoverageRowType, CoverageStatus, LeanCoverageMatrix, LEAN_COVERAGE_SCHEMA_VERSION,
 };
 use serde_json::Value;
 use std::collections::BTreeSet;
@@ -21,59 +21,43 @@ fn sample_matrix_parses_and_validates() {
 #[test]
 fn sample_matrix_contains_all_required_row_types() {
     let matrix = LeanCoverageMatrix::from_json_str(SAMPLE_JSON).expect("sample must parse");
-    assert!(
-        matrix
-            .rows
-            .iter()
-            .any(|row| row.row_type == CoverageRowType::SemanticRule)
-    );
-    assert!(
-        matrix
-            .rows
-            .iter()
-            .any(|row| row.row_type == CoverageRowType::Invariant)
-    );
-    assert!(
-        matrix
-            .rows
-            .iter()
-            .any(|row| row.row_type == CoverageRowType::RefinementObligation)
-    );
-    assert!(
-        matrix
-            .rows
-            .iter()
-            .any(|row| row.row_type == CoverageRowType::OperationalGate)
-    );
+    assert!(matrix
+        .rows
+        .iter()
+        .any(|row| row.row_type == CoverageRowType::SemanticRule));
+    assert!(matrix
+        .rows
+        .iter()
+        .any(|row| row.row_type == CoverageRowType::Invariant));
+    assert!(matrix
+        .rows
+        .iter()
+        .any(|row| row.row_type == CoverageRowType::RefinementObligation));
+    assert!(matrix
+        .rows
+        .iter()
+        .any(|row| row.row_type == CoverageRowType::OperationalGate));
 }
 
 #[test]
 fn sample_matrix_contains_status_model_examples() {
     let matrix = LeanCoverageMatrix::from_json_str(SAMPLE_JSON).expect("sample must parse");
-    assert!(
-        matrix
-            .rows
-            .iter()
-            .any(|row| row.status == CoverageStatus::InProgress)
-    );
-    assert!(
-        matrix
-            .rows
-            .iter()
-            .any(|row| row.status == CoverageStatus::Blocked)
-    );
-    assert!(
-        matrix
-            .rows
-            .iter()
-            .any(|row| row.status == CoverageStatus::Proven)
-    );
-    assert!(
-        matrix
-            .rows
-            .iter()
-            .any(|row| row.status == CoverageStatus::ValidatedInCi)
-    );
+    assert!(matrix
+        .rows
+        .iter()
+        .any(|row| row.status == CoverageStatus::InProgress));
+    assert!(matrix
+        .rows
+        .iter()
+        .any(|row| row.status == CoverageStatus::Blocked));
+    assert!(matrix
+        .rows
+        .iter()
+        .any(|row| row.status == CoverageStatus::Proven));
+    assert!(matrix
+        .rows
+        .iter()
+        .any(|row| row.status == CoverageStatus::ValidatedInCi));
 }
 
 #[test]
